reasoning: The market has predicted 86% historically. The game enters Early Access on March 5, 2026, meaning resolution will be on March 19. Slay the Spire has massive community goodwill, an overwhelmingly positive rating on the original game (97%), and earned praised for ditching Unity for Godot. These point toward a very strong launch. However, requiring >=95% positive reviews leaves almost no margin for error. The recent announcement of 4-player co-op introduces a non-trivial risk of technical issues/crashes at launch, which historically drives early negative reviews in Early Access titles. In addition, some minor fan disappointment surrounding the label of 'iterative sequel' or missing characters like 'The Defect' in early trailers could add up to 2-3% of negative reviews. Taking the base strength of the STS franchise into account while allowing for slightly more bug/iteration blowback, a probability of 82% balances the extremely robust hype with the strictness of the 95% threshold.
